bottom_line: Tighten the functional/design contract for default-sort UI state and pagination URL preservation; this mismatch caused the only substantive fix cycle.
coverage_notes: All I-00095 logs were below 1 MB, so they were reviewed directly with targeted grep and line-level evidence extraction; DB telemetry available (db-identity check: UP).

findings.title: Functional-spec drift on default sort indicator and pagination URL preservation
findings.recommendation: Add explicit invariants to the functional template describing the distinction between backend default ordering and visible active sort state, and define pagination sort-param preservation semantics.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00095_S09_run1.log:551 - I-00095_Functional.md says default load has no highlighted header, but implementation defaults to sort=created_at, dir=desc, and template marks timestamp as active with chevron.
ai-dev/logs/I-00095_S09_fix1.log:380 - default (no sort in URL) now keeps the backend default ordering but shows no active sort indicator
ai-dev/logs/I-00095_S09_fix1.log:391 - Made Prev/Next preserve sort only when sort is explicitly in the URL.
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Clarify sortable-table URL/UI invariants in the functional template so default ordering and active-sort visualization are unambiguous; context: I-00095 self-assessment in ai-dev/active/I-00095/reports/I-00095_self_assess_report.md.
